cards.definition: Rust's system for tracking memory: each value has one owner at a time
Letting code temporarily use a value without taking ownership
A variable declared with `mut` that allows changing its value
Annotations that describe the scope for which a reference is valid
A collection of methods that define shared behavior (like interfaces)
Rust’s build system and package manager
A custom data type that lets you name and package together multiple related values
A type that can be any one of several variants
A mechanism that allows comparing a value against a series of patterns
An enum that represents a value that can be either something or nothing
An enum that represents either success (Ok) or failure (Err)
A compilation unit in Rust (either a library or an executable)
A way to organize code into a hierarchy for readability and reuse
Code that bypasses some of Rust's safety checks (e.g., raw pointers)
High-level features that compile to code as efficient as hand-written low-level code
